segments.text: Now King David was old, advanced in years; and they put covers on him, but he could not get warm.
Therefore his servants said to him,
Let a young woman, a virgin, be sought for our lord the king, and let her stand before the king, and let her care for him; and let her lie in your bosom, that our lord the king may be warm.
segments.speaker: King's Servants

segments.text: So they sought for a lovely young woman throughout all the territory of Israel, and found Abishag the Shunammite, and brought her to the king.
The young woman was very lovely; and she cared for the king, and served him; but the king did not know her.
Then Adonijah the son of Haggith exalted himself,
I will be king
segments.speaker: Adonijah

segments.text: and he prepared for himself chariots and horsemen, and fifty men to run before him.
(And his father had not rebuked him at any time by saying, 'Why have you done so?' He was also very good-looking. His mother had borne him after Absalom.)
Then he conferred with Joab the son of Zeruiah and with Abiathar the priest, and they followed and helped Adonijah.
But Zadok the priest, Benaiah the son of Jehoiada, Nathan the prophet, Shimei, Rei, and the mighty men who belonged to David were not with Adonijah.
So Nathan spoke to Bathsheba the mother of Solomon, saying,
Have you not heard that Adonijah the son of Haggith has become king, and David our lord does not know it?
segments.speaker: Nathan

segments.text: Come, please, let me now give you advice, that you may save your own life and the life of your son Solomon.
segments.speaker: Nathan

segments.text: Go immediately to King David and say to him, 'Did you not, my lord, O king, swear to your maidservant, saying, "Assuredly your son Solomon shall reign after me, and he shall sit on my throne"? Why then has Adonijah become king?'
segments.speaker: Nathan

segments.text: And Bathsheba bowed and did homage to the king.
Then the king said,
What is your wish?
segments.speaker: King David

segments.text: Then she said to him,
My lord, you swore by the Lord your God to your maidservant, saying, 'Assuredly Solomon your son shall reign after me, and he shall sit on my throne.'
segments.speaker: Bathsheba

segments.text: And Nathan said,
My lord, O king, have you said, 'Adonijah shall reign after me, and he shall sit on my throne'?
segments.speaker: Nathan

segments.text: Then King David answered and said,
Call Bathsheba to me.
segments.speaker: King David

segments.text: So she came into the king's presence and stood before the king.
And the king took an oath and said,
As the Lord lives, who has redeemed my life from every distress,
segments.speaker: King David

segments.text: Then Bathsheba bowed with her face to the earth, and paid homage to the king,
Let my lord King David live forever!
segments.speaker: Bathsheba

segments.text: Benaiah the son of Jehoiada answered the king and said,
Amen! May the Lord God of my lord the king say so too.
segments.speaker: Benaiah

segments.text: While he was still speaking, there came Jonathan, the son of Abiathar the priest.
And Adonijah said to him,
Come in, for you are a prominent man, and bring good news.
segments.speaker: Adonijah

segments.text: Then Jonathan answered and said to Adonijah,
No! Our lord King David has made Solomon king.
segments.speaker: Jonathan

segments.text: Then Solomon said,
If he proves himself a worthy man, not one hair of him shall fall to the earth; but if wickedness is found in him, he shall die.
segments.speaker: Solomon
